 {"id":"bd-2z7a","title":"Implement dynamic model creation","description":"## Description

Create models dynamically at runtime.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
from typing import Optional

def create_model(name: str, fields: dict):
    return type(name, (SQLModel,), {
        '__annotations__': fields,
        '__tablename__': name.lower(),
    })

DynamicUser = create_model('DynamicUser', {
    'id': Optional[int],
    'name': str,
})
```

## Rust Approach

Rust's static typing makes true dynamic models impossible, but we can:

### Option 1: Generic Dynamic Model
```rust
pub struct DynamicModel {
    table_name: String,
    columns: HashMap<String, ColumnDef>,
    values: HashMap<String, Value>,
}

impl DynamicModel {
    pub fn new(table_name: &str) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn add_column(&mut self, name: &str, def: ColumnDef) { ... }
    pub fn set(&mut self, name: &str, value: Value) { ... }
    pub fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&Value> { ... }
}
```

### Option 2: Schema from database
```rust
let schema = introspect_table(cx, conn, "users").await?;
let dynamic = DynamicModel::from_schema(schema);
```

## Use Cases

- Database introspection tools
- Generic admin interfaces
- Schema migration tools

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DynamicModel type
- [ ] Runtime column definition
- [ ] Query execution with dynamic models
- [ ] Serialization to/from JSON
- [ ] Table creation from dynamic model

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/dynamic.rs)
- [ ] Test create_model function
- [ ] Test field definitions at runtime
- [ ] Test validators on dynamic models
- [ ] Test inheritance for dynamic models

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/dynamic_models.rs)
- [ ] create_model("User", [("name", str)]) works
- [ ] Dynamic model validates correctly
- [ ] Dynamic model serializes correctly
- [ ] Dynamic model in database operations
- [ ] Performance vs static models

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Dynamic model creation
- [ ] TRACE: Field definition processing

 {"id":"bd-2zf","title":"Implement IndeterminateSpinner for unknown-length operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a spinner renderable for operations where we do not know the total count or duration, showing activity with elapsed time.

## Background
Many database operations have unknown duration:
- Establishing connections
- Running complex queries
- Waiting for locks
- Initial data discovery

Users need feedback that something is happening, even without a completion percentage.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/spinner.rs

### Core Struct
IndeterminateSpinner holds:
- message: String
- started_at: Instant
- style: SpinnerStyle enum
- theme: Theme

### SpinnerStyle Enum
- Dots: ... (three dots cycling)
- Braille: Unicode braille pattern animation
- Line: -/|\ rotating
- Arrow: arrow rotating
- Simple: asterisk blinking

### Frame Generation
Each style defines frames array and interval_ms.
Example Dots: [".", "..", "...", ".."] at 250ms intervals

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
^---- spinner animation
     ^---- message
                           ^---- elapsed time

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
(Static output, no animation - agents see single line)

## API Design
IndeterminateSpinner::new(message)
  .style(SpinnerStyle::Dots)
  .theme(theme)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

### Convert to Progress
When length becomes known, convert spinner to progress:
let progress = spinner.into_progress(total);

### Live Update Pattern
let spinner = IndeterminateSpinner::new("Connecting");
console.start_spinner(&spinner);
// ... do work
console.stop_spinner(&spinner);

## Verification Steps
1. Test each spinner style
2. Verify elapsed time formatting (s, m:ss)
3. Test conversion to progress bar
4. Verify plain text output
5. Test with long messages (truncation)
6. Verify animation timing

## Dependencies
- Theme from this crate
- std::time::Instant for timing","acceptance_criteria":"IndeterminateSpinner shows activity animation
Spinner has multiple style options
Spinner respects terminal width
Plain mode outputs dots or status text
All unit tests verify animation frames
Performance tests confirm low overhead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:38.251317294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436765300Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436722440Z","close_reason":"Implemented IndeterminateSpinner with 5 styles, conversion to progress bar, and 20 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-2zf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_spinner_creation - verify initial state
2. test_spinner_all_styles - verify each SpinnerStyle
3. test_spinner_frame_generation - verify animation frames
4. test_spinner_elapsed_time - verify duration tracking
5. test_spinner_render_plain - verify text output
6. test_spinner_message_update - verify message changes
7. test_spinner_convert_to_progress - verify conversion
8. test_spinner_render_rich - verify rich output (with feature)","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:07Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-30g6","title":"console: make ErrorPanel render_styled width-safe and Unicode-safe","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found panic risks in ErrorPanel::render_styled: (1) width math uses unchecked subtraction/repeat counts and can underflow for tiny widths, (2) SQL truncation slices by byte index and can panic on non-ASCII boundaries. Fix by clamping minimum render width and using char-safe truncation paths. Add regression tests for tiny width and Unicode SQL truncation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:29:41.067771290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:31:28.329555656Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T07:31:28.329491466Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30g6","depends_on_id":"bd-162","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:29:41.084609969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-318","title":"Phase 8: Facade Crate Integration and Public API","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the console system into the main sqlmodel facade crate, providing a unified public API for console configuration and usage across all sqlmodel components.

## Background
The sqlmodel facade crate is the main entry point for users. It must:
- Re-export console types and traits
- Provide easy console configuration
- Integrate console with high-level operations
- Maintain backward compatibility for users not using console

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Console Re-exports in sqlmodel Prelude
Add to prelude:
- SqlModelConsole
- OutputMode
- Theme
- ConsoleAware trait
- Key renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)

### 2. Console Builder Integration
Extend existing builders to accept console:
Session::builder()
    .with_console(console)
    .connect(url)

### 3. Global Console Support
Optional global console for convenience:
sqlmodel::set_global_console(console);
// Now all operations use this console automatically

### 4. Configuration API
Easy configuration without deep imports:
use sqlmodel::prelude::*;

let console = SqlModelConsole::builder()
    .auto_detect_mode()  // agent vs human
    .theme(Theme::dark())
    .build();

### 5. Feature Flag Coordination
Ensure console feature properly propagates:
- sqlmodel enables sqlmodel-console when console feature active
- Drivers enable their console support when console active
- All is optional, no bloat for non-console users

## Integration Points
- Session/connection builders
- Query execution methods
- Error handling paths
- Schema operations

## Agent Safety Preserved
The facade maintains agent safety guarantees:
- Default is auto-detect mode
- Plain mode always available
- No breaking changes to stdout semantics

## Dependencies
- Phase 2-7 complete (all console infrastructure ready)
- All driver integrations complete

## Verification
- Test prelude imports work
- Test builder integration
- Test global console pattern
- Verify feature flags work correctly","acceptance_criteria":"sqlmodel prelude re-exports console types when feature enabled
Session and Connection builders accept console configuration
Global console support works for convenience usage
Feature-gated compilation works correctly
All unit tests pass for facade integration
Documentation shows correct usage patterns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:06.150467154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471189789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471146628Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-88i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":48,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Console types exported in sqlmodel prelude
- [ ] Session::builder().with_console() works
- [ ] Global console via sqlmodel::set_global_console()
- [ ] Console propagates from session to connection
- [ ] Feature flag 'console' properly gates all functionality
- [ ] No compile errors without console feature
- [ ] Integration tests verify API surface","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:34Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed thread-safety issue in global console by removing the rich_rust::Console field from SqlModelConsole (which contained Cell/RefCell types that are not Sync). The rich console functionality is still available but will be created on-demand rather than stored. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ed","title":"Fix lossy f32 conversions — add precision-loss detection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:59:02.328408916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:28:09.402978274Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:28:09.402913132Z","close_reason":"Added precision loss detection to TryFrom<Value> for f32/f64 and FromValue for f32/f64, plus to_f32_lossy()/to_f64_lossy() helpers","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ed","depends_on_id":"bd-9a25","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-31ed","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What

Add precision-loss detection to TryFrom<Value> for f32 and FromValue for f32.

## Where

Files:
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs, lines 408-423 (TryFrom<Value> for f32)
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs, lines 408-425 (FromValue for f32)

## Current Code (value.rs)

```rust
impl TryFrom<Value> for f32 {
    type Error = Error;
    fn try_from(value: Value) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
        match value {
            Value::Float(v) => Ok(v),
            #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
            Value::Double(v) => Ok(v as f32),  // LOSSY
            #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
            Value::Int(v) => Ok(v as f32),     // LOSSY for v > 16_777_216
            #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
            Value::BigInt(v) => Ok(v as f32),  // VERY LOSSY
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

## Fix

Add range/precision checks:
```rust
Value::Double(v) => {
    let converted = v as f32;
    if (converted as f64 - v).abs() > f64::EPSILON * v.abs().max(1.0) {
        return Err(Error::Type(TypeError {
            expected: "f32-representable value",
            actual: format!("f64 {} loses precision as f32", v),
            column: None,
            rust_type: Some("f32"),
        }));
    }
    Ok(converted)
}
Value::Int(v) => {
    if v.unsigned_abs() > (1 << 24) {
        return Err(Error::Type(TypeError {
            expected: "f32-representable integer",
            actual: format!("i32 {} exceeds f32 exact integer range", v),
            column: None,
            rust_type: Some("f32"),
        }));
    }
    Ok(v as f32)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- f64→f32 conversion errors when precision is lost
- i32→f32 conversion errors when |value| > 2^24
- i64→f32 conversion errors when |value| > 2^24
- Existing valid conversions still work
- Same fixes applied to both value.rs TryFrom and row.rs FromValue
","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:59:02Z"},{"id":51,"issue_id":"bd-31ed","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## REFINEMENT: Opt-In Lossy vs Strict Conversion

### The User Experience Problem

Some users legitimately want lossy f32 conversions — for example, reading a DOUBLE column into an f32 field where they know the values are small. Making TryFrom strict by default is correct, but we should provide an escape hatch.

### Recommended Approach: Strict by Default, Lossy Helper

1. **TryFrom<Value> for f32** — STRICT (errors on precision loss). This is the default.

2. **Add a helper method on Value** — for users who accept lossy conversion:
```rust
impl Value {
    /// Convert to f32, allowing precision loss.
    /// Returns an error only for completely unrepresentable values (NaN, infinity).
    pub fn to_f32_lossy(&self) -> Result<f32, Error> { ... }

    /// Convert to f64, allowing precision loss for large integers.
    pub fn to_f64_lossy(&self) -> Result<f64, Error> { ... }
}
```

This gives users a clear choice:
- `value.try_into::<f32>()?` — strict, errors on precision loss
- `value.to_f32_lossy()?` — lenient, only errors on impossible conversions

### Scope Clarification: row.rs Must Match

The `FromValue` trait in row.rs is the OTHER path values take (database → Rust struct). It must use the same strict logic. Users who want lossy behavior in their model fields should use a wrapper type or handle it in their deserialize impl.

### Precision Loss Thresholds

For f32:
- i32 values: exact up to ±16,777,216 (2^24)
- i64 values: exact up to ±16,777,216 (same limit)
- f64 values: exact if the value is representable as f32 (check: `v as f32 as f64 == v`)

For f64:
- i64 values: exact up to ±9,007,199,254,740,992 (2^53)

### Testing Note

Test both the strict path (TryFrom) and the lossy helper (to_f32_lossy):
- Strict: verify errors at boundaries
- Lossy: verify success at same boundaries, errors only for infinity/NaN
","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:22Z"},{"id":52,"issue_id":"bd-31ed","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SCOPE: This task MUST update BOTH files:
1. crates/sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs (TryFrom<Value> for f32) 
2. crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs (FromValue for f32)
Both paths must have identical precision checks. Also add to_f32_lossy() and to_f64_lossy() helpers on Value for users who want lenient conversions.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31l","title":"Implement Session.get() for primary key lookup","description":"## Description

Get object by primary key, checking identity map first.

## Python Behavior

```python
user = session.get(User, 1)  # Get User with id=1
user = session.get(User, (1, 2))  # Composite PK

# With options:
user = session.get(User, 1, with_for_update=True)
user = session.get(User, 1, options=[joinedload(User.addresses)])
```

Key behavior:
1. Check identity map first (return cached if exists)
2. Query database if not in identity map
3. Return None if not found

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn get<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        pk: impl Into<PrimaryKey>,
    ) -> Outcome<Option<M>, Error> {
        // 1. Check identity map
        if let Some(cached) = self.identity_map.get::<M>(&pk) {
            return Ok(Some(cached.clone()));
        }
        
        // 2. Query database
        let result = select!(M)
            .filter(M::primary_key().eq(pk))
            .first(cx, &self.conn)
            .await?;
        
        // 3. Add to identity map if found
        if let Some(ref model) = result {
            self.identity_map.insert(model);
        }
        
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Returns from identity map if present
- [ ] Queries DB if not in identity map
- [ ] Adds to identity map after DB fetch
- [ ] Returns None if not found
- [ ] Works with composite primary keys
- [ ] with_for_update option supported","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:03.803192332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:09:51.737374394Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:09:51.736912393Z","close_reason":"Added get_by_pk, get_with_options, and GetOptions with FOR UPDATE support","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-2pkb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-emz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
